book_claims.claim: Contain the highest concentration of beta-carotene among common vegetables
book_claims.mechanism: 31mg beta-carotene per 100g cooked, converted to vitamin A for eye health and immune function

supplementary_research.finding: Orange-fleshed sweet potato consumption significantly improved vitamin A status in children in sub-Saharan Africa. A food-based approach that confirms the book's claim about beta-carotene to vitamin A conversion.

Sweet potato is a dietary staple in Okinawa, one of the world's Blue Zones. The traditional Okinawan diet derived ~67% of calories from sweet potato and is associated with exceptional longevity and low rates of cancer and heart disease.
White-skinned sweet potato extract (Caiapo) improved insulin sensitivity and glycemic control in type 2 diabetes patients in a randomized controlled trial.
Sweet potatoes have a moderate glycemic index (~63) but their glycemic load depends heavily on preparation. Boiling produces a lower GI than baking. High in fiber, beta-carotene, and potassium.

texture: crispy when roasted at high heat

culinary_pairings.source: Fat essential for beta-carotene absorption; roasted sweet potato wedges with EVOO
Complementary amino acids and flavors; sweet potato and black bean tacos/bowls
Ginger cuts sweetness and adds anti-inflammatory synergy; sweet potato-ginger curry
Warm spice complements natural sweetness in Moroccan-style sweet potato dishes
Acid balances sweetness; classic in Thai sweet potato curry and Mexican preparations
Creamy fat source aids carotenoid absorption; roasted sweet potato with tahini drizzle
Complementary nutrients: sweet potato provides beta-carotene, kale provides vitamin K and sulforaphane

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highest of any common vegetable. Cooking breaks cell walls and increases bioavailability 3-6x. Fat co-ingestion is essential. Orange-fleshed varieties have the most.
A single medium sweet potato provides >100% RDA. Conversion from beta-carotene is regulated, preventing toxicity.
Mix of soluble and resistant starch (increases when cooked and cooled). Resistant starch feeds beneficial gut bacteria.
Important for blood pressure regulation. Sweet potatoes provide more potassium than bananas per serving.
Purple sweet potatoes contain anthocyanins with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties distinct from orange varieties' carotenoids.
